body: `WarmUp` only needs pivot keys to populate `b.mx` nodes, but was calling `dataLookupFunc` which reads and decompresses both key and value. Switch to `Reset` + `Next(key[:0])` + `Skip` so the value is never decoded.
title: btindex: WarmUp: read key-only, skip value to avoid unnecessary decompression

body:
```
Cherry-pick of #21830 to `main`.

Switch `RecSplit.bucketCollector` from `LargeSortableBuffers` to `SmallSortableBuffers` to reduce peak memory usage during `.bt` index builds.

The original squash diff also added an unused `newIndexFromMemory` helper that depends on the `Index.readers` `sync.Pool` field present only on the `performance` branch; it is omitted here so the change builds on `main`.
```

```
body:
added new api env.SetMaxFreelistReuse. 
Docs for new api: https://github.com/ledgerwatch/lmdb-go/releases/tag/v1.16.0 

Right now: 
```
LMDBDefaultMaxFreelistReuse = uint(10_000) // measured in pages
```
Need test if this value is too big or not, and reduce if need
Because setting it too low - in my intuition can lead to db growth: 
```
1. You have 100K pages freelist record
2. You use some small amount of pages from there
3. You can't reuse any freelist and allocate new 100K pages (db growth) 
4. On next cycle it grows again - and old 100K pages gone to freelist 
5. On next cycle allocate new 100K pages - and send old 100K pages to freelist
6. .... profit!
```

-----
Reference for last experiment from Discord:  https://discordapp.com/channels/687972960811745322/687972960811745326/764059490835365908

```
0x6007bb0 [1602235779:723984] mdb_cursor_put - before mdb_node

```
body:
Fixes CR comment from #19788.

In `searchUpperReverse`, the `n <= 0` fast path (when `hiIdx == lo`) returned `lo`, causing `searchReverse` to still probe `idx=0` on guaranteed-miss queries. The correct return is `lo + 1`, mirroring `searchUpperForward` and ensuring the caller loop is skipped entirely when there is no solution.
title: db/recsplit/eliasfano32: fix searchUpperReverse off-by-one on no-solution fast path

body:
golang's stdlib has `index/suffixarray` package with same algo
But C version does `malloc` calls (all syscalls from C code are not very friendly for Go's runtime - because thread-freezing)

```
benchstat old.txt new.txt
goos: linux
goarch: amd64
pkg: github.com/erigontech/erigon/db/seg/sais
cpu: AMD EPYC 4344P 8-Core Processor
                 │   old.txt    │              new.txt               │
                 │    sec/op    │   sec/op     vs base               │
Sais/1024-16        8.575µ ± 2%   7.722µ ± 1%  -9.95% (p=0.000 n=10)
Sais/65536-16       1.796m ± 2%   1.719m ± 1%  -4.29% (p=0.000 n=10)
Sais/1048576-16     31.37m ± 1%   30.65m ± 2%  -2.29% (p=0.001 n=10)
Sais/16777216-16    1.205 ± 5%    1.016 ± 16%  -15.64% (p=0.000 n=10)

```
we run it on 16Mb super-strings

tested on several real 10gb files - checksum not changed

body:
bench heap-alloc vs buf-alloc
```
cpu: Apple M4 Max
BenchmarkDecompressNext/buf-16         	   10000	    114715 ns/op	       0 B/op	       0 allocs/op
BenchmarkDecompressNext/heap-16        	    8990	    129304 ns/op	   31921 B/op	    3000 allocs/op

```
speed difference is 10% - on non-concurrent workload (heap also has own mutex - so, likely it's throughput will degrade on concurrent bench)

body:
- can open old files
- does increase major version of `.efi`, `commitment.kvi` (`txn.idx`, `header.idx` - will continue use prev version until some future PRs)
- does use `fuse_filter` lib (analog of bloom filter)
- doesn't require `enum=true` (prev version did require)
- changing "hot dataset" from `5bytes/key` to `9bits/key`
- `commitment.kvi` - seems I succeed to set `enum=false`
 

Unfortunately: 
- can't set `enum=false` to `.efi`: because `g.BinarySearch(hi.from, n, idx.OrdinalLookup)`
- can't set `enum=false` to `txn.idx`: because `txNumInFile, ok := reader.Lookup(txnHash[:])` (and `txNumInFile` returned to user)
- can't set `enum=false` to `header.idx`: because `index.OrdinalLookup(blockHeight - index.BaseDataID())`
- I did increase major version of `txs.seg` - because we don't support separated versions for `.seg` and `.idx` (will create ticket)

I didn't use dedic
```

body:
Change from: 
```
begin(TxNoSync)
exec new block, index nee data
do limited pruning 
commit()
send notifications about new data arrival to other apps, they may start reading new data at this time (by new read transactions) 
```
Change to: 
```
begin(TxNoSync)
exec new block, index nee data
commit() // no fsync here
send notifications about new data arrival to other apps, they may start reading new data at this time (by new read transactions) 
begin()
do pruning 
commit() // fsync here
```

it allows notify earlier. Fsync (of all changes) on modern drives is fast, but on cloud-drives it’s about 1sec in worst cases.
title: Exec new blocks in TxnNoSync. Prune after sending notifications in TxnSync
